summary:
- Left 4th pharyngeal arch contributes predominantly to the development of part of the left common carotid artery which affects the left subclavian artery but not directly; it also impacts the left aortic arch.
- Right 4th pharyngeal arch is crucial for forming the initial segment of the right subclavian artery without direct involvement with other specified arterial structures mentioned as distractors.
```
